chunk_summary: Clinic vignettes illustrate sequenced outpatient pathways for managing pediatric obesity, emphasizing comorbidity-driven decisions such as insulin resistance, OSA, steatotic liver disease, and depression screening.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of comorbidities in tailoring obesity management pathways for pediatric patients.
Apply evidence-based guidelines for screening and managing insulin resistance, OSA, and steatotic liver disease in children with obesity.
Recognize the importance of mental health assessment in obesity management, especially in cases involving bullying or depression.
key_facts: A BMI above the adult-equivalent 23 line on the IAP 2015 chart indicates overweight status in Indian practice.
A 12-year-old with obesity and habitual snoring should be evaluated for OSA using polysomnography or ENT referral per AAP guidelines.
Elevated ALT in a 14-year-old with class 2 obesity suggests steatotic liver disease risk; repeat ALT in 8–12 weeks and refer if persistent.
Mental health linkage and safety assessment are prioritized for a 13-year-old with obesity and school avoidance due to bullying.
Lifestyle interventions include removing sugar-sweetened beverages, restructuring meals, and protecting sleep routines.
reference_quote: A BMI above the adult-equivalent 23 line on the IAP 2015 chart indicates overweight status in Indian practice. (Source: IAP Guidelines on Prevention and Management of Childhood Obesity, 2023)
chunk_heading: OVERNUTRITION & OBESITY MANAGEMENT;SEQUENCED OUTPATIENT PATHWAY AND FOLLOW-UP CADENCE;CLINIC VIGNETTES ILLUSTRATING PATHWAY-CONSISTENT DECISIONS;
subtopic: SEQUENCED OUTPATIENT PATHWAY AND FOLLOW-UP CADENCE
